title: 11 Herbal Oils for Older Adults to Reduce Swollen Gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils, also known as tea tree oil, have been used for centuries to treat various oral health issues, including swollen gums. This oil is rich in properties and constituents that make it effective against swollen gums. The main compounds in Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils, such as terpinen-4-ol, cineole, and limonene, have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties that help to reduce swelling, fight infection, and soothe irritated tissues. When you apply a few drops of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils to your swollen gums, it helps to decrease the inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause the infection. To use Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils for swollen gums, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected area using a cotton swab. You can also add a few drops of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils to your toothpaste or mouthwash to prevent future infections. As you continue to use Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils, you can expect to see a reduction in swelling, a decrease in pain, and an improvement in your overall oral health. Your gums may start to look and feel healthier, and you may be able to prevent future infections by regularly using Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils are known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swollen gums. This is due to the presence of compounds such as alkylamides and glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. These constituents can help to reduce the swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and chemicals. To use Echinacea purpurea essential oils for swollen gums, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected area using a cotton swab. You can also add a few drops of the oil to your toothbrush or mouthwash for added benefits. However, it's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea essential oils should not be ingested or used undiluted, as this can cause irritation and other adverse effects. When you apply the Echinacea purpurea essential oils to your swollen gums, you may start to see a reduction in swelling and pain within a few hours. The inflammation may also start to subside, and the gums may begin to feel softer and less tender. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of the oil may help to prevent further infection and promote healing. However, it's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea essential oils are not a replacement for medical treatment, and you should consult with a healthcare professional if your symptoms persist or worsen.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils have been traditionally used to help with swollen gums due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil contains constituents like thujone, camphor, and borneol, which have been shown to reduce swelling and kill bacteria that can cause gum infections. When gums become swollen, it's often a sign of infection or irritation, and Salvia officinalis essential oils can help alleviate this by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.  To use Salvia officinalis essential oils for swollen gums, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected area using a cotton swab. You can also add a few drops to your toothpaste or mouthwash for a more direct application. It's essential to dilute the oil properly, as it can be quite potent and may cause skin irritation if applied undiluted. When you apply Salvia officinalis essential oils to swollen gums, you can expect to see a reduction in swelling and pain within a few hours. The oil's antimicrobial properties will help combat any underlying infection, promoting a healthy environment for your gums to heal. Regular application may also help prevent future infections and keep your gums healthy. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's crucial to consult with a dentist or healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contain compounds like eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the gums. When used in a gentle dental massage, the oil can also ease pain and discomfort in the affected area. The menthol in Eucalyptus globulus essential oils acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the pain and making it more manageable.  To use Eucalyptus globulus essential oils for swollen gums, dilute a few drops in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage it onto the affected area using a circular motion. You can also add a few drops to a warm saltwater rinse to reduce inflammation and ease pain. When you use Eucalyptus globulus essential oils in this way, you may experience a reduction in swelling and pain, as well as a decrease in the redness and inflammation associated with gum problems. Your gums may feel cooler and less tender, making it easier to eat and speak without discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils, also known as ginger essential oil, is a natural remedy that can help alleviate swollen gums. It contains properties and constituents such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that can cause gum infections. When used topically, ginger essential oil can be applied to the affected area using a cotton swab or added to a warm saltwater rinse to help soothe and calm swollen gums. To use ginger essential oil for swollen gums, start by diluting a few drops of the oil in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Then, gently massage the oil mixture onto your gums using a cotton swab or your finger. You can also add a few drops of ginger essential oil to a warm saltwater rinse and swish it around your mouth before spitting it out. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for best results. When you take action and use ginger essential oil for swollen gums, you can expect to see a reduction in inflammation and pain. The antimicrobial properties of the oil will help to fight off bacteria that can cause gum infections, promoting a healthy balance of bacteria in your mouth. As the inflammation subsides, you may notice an improvement in your overall oral health, with less bleeding and discomfort when eating or drinking. With regular use, ginger essential oil can help to prevent future gum infections and promote a healthy, happy smile.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils, also known as peppermint oil, can be beneficial for swollen gums due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It contains constituents like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which have natural pain-relieving and soothing effects. When applied topically to the affected area, peppermint oil can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort.  To use peppermint oil for swollen gums, gently swish a few drops of the oil in your mouth for about 30 seconds to allow the oil to reach the affected area. Then, spit it out and rinse your mouth with warm water. You can also mix a few drops of peppermint oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to your gums using a cotton swab.  Applying peppermint oil to swollen gums may lead to a decrease in swelling and pain. The menthol in the oil can also help numb the area, providing temporary relief. However, it's essential to note that peppermint oil may not address the underlying cause of the gum swelling, so it's recommended to consult a dentist or healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. Regular use of peppermint oil may also lead to improved oral health and reduced inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swollen gums. This is due to its high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals. When applied topically, these compounds can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. If you're experiencing swollen gums, try mixing a few drops of Calendula officinalis essential oils with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and gently apply it to the affected area with a cotton swab. You can also add a few drops to your toothpaste or mouthwash for added benefits. However, be sure to dilute the essential oils properly, as they can be quite potent. When you apply Calendula officinalis essential oils to swollen gums, you can expect to see a reduction in inflammation and swelling within a few hours. The pain and discomfort should also start to subside, making it easier to eat and speak. In some cases, the gums may even start to return to their normal color and texture.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, have been traditionally used to help with swollen gums. The oil's active compounds, including cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and linalool, are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Cinnamaldehyde, in particular, has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum infections. When gums are swollen, it can be painful and uncomfortable, but using Cinnamomum verum essential oils may help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. To use Cinnamomum verum essential oils for swollen gums, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage it onto your gums. You can also add a few drops of the oil to a warm saltwater rinse and swish it around your mouth before spitting it out. Some people find it helpful to apply a small amount of the oil to a cotton ball and place it on the affected area. When using Cinnamomum verum essential oils, it's essential to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose as needed. Using Cinnamomum verum essential oils for swollen gums may lead to a reduction in swelling and pain, making it more comfortable to eat and speak. It may also help to prevent further inflammation and gum disease by killing bacteria and reducing inflammation. However, it's essential to note that Cinnamomum verum essential oils should not replace regular dental care and should not be used as a substitute for professional medical advice.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which make them a great natural remedy for swollen gums. The oil contains compounds like quinine and quinidine, which have been shown to reduce swelling and pain. These constituents help to prevent the release of histamine, a chemical that causes inflammation, and also have a numbing effect on the gums.  When using Cinchona officinalis essential oils for swollen gums, start by mixing a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and gently massage it onto your gums. You can also add a few drops to your toothbrush and brush your teeth as usual. It's recommended to use a low concentration of the oil, as it can be quite potent. Applying the oil to your gums may help reduce swelling and pain within a few minutes. You may also notice a decrease in inflammation and sensitivity over time. However, if your swollen gums are a symptom of a more serious condition, such as gum disease or an infection, you should consult a dentist or a healthcare professional for proper treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils, also known as lemongrass oil, contains properties and constituents that help alleviate swollen gums. The main active compounds, citral and limonene, have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that target the root cause of gum swelling. When applied topically, these compounds reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause infections.  To use Cymbopogon citratus essential oils for swollen gums, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected area with a cotton swab. Gently massage it into the gums for a few minutes. You can also add it to your toothpaste or mouthwash for an added antibacterial effect. As you apply the oil, you can expect the swelling to reduce, and the pain to subside. The inflammation should decrease, allowing you to eat and speak comfortably. The antibacterial properties will help prevent further infections, and the anti-inflammatory effects will promote healing and soothe the gums.  Remember to always dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil and do a patch test before using it, especially if you're new to using essential oils.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for swollen gums

intro_desc: As people get older, their gums can become swollen and inflamed due to various reasons such as poor oral hygiene, gum disease, or even certain medications. This condition, known as gingivitis or periodontitis, can cause discomfort, pain, and difficulty eating, drinking, and speaking. Older adults may also experience bad breath, bleeding gums, and even tooth loss. This can significantly affect their quality of life, making everyday tasks a challenge. Swollen gums can also be a sign of a more serious underlying condition, such as diabetes or heart disease, which requires immediate medical attention. As a result, older adults often seek alternative remedies to alleviate their symptoms and improve their oral health.  When considering herbal essential oils for swollen gums, it's essential to note that these oils are not a replacement for medical treatment. However, they can be used in conjunction with dental care to help reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote healing. These oils are typically derived from plants and contain potent compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. When applied topically to the affected area, they can help to soothe and calm the swollen gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing to occur.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for swollen gums
